key_insight: GCPO replaces uniform sample-level rewards with per-token advantages by contrasting model predictions under positive and negative prompts, giving finer credit assignment for discrete policy optimization.
engineering_relevance: This enables more targeted and scalable training signals for reasoning and generation systems, improving optimization quality in tasks where only a subset of tokens truly drive performance.
method: Compute contrastive predictions using positive vs. negative prompts.
Assign token-level advantages from the prediction differences instead of broadcasting one reward to all tokens.
Optimize a discrete policy with these finer-grained credit signals.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports extensive experiments showing consistent gains over GRPO and DAPO on both text-to-image generation and chain-of-thought reasoning benchmarks.
watch_item: The method depends on constructing meaningful positive/negative prompts, so performance may be sensitive to prompt design and contrast quality.
paper_title: Guidance Contrastive Token Credit Assignment for Discrete Policy Optimization
